title: Implement parallel file load in Rust

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Linked story: virt-689472
Goal: parallelize Rust `Table::load_from_dir` (and/or `Database::from_schema`) without changing behavior.
Benchmark gate: compare 10k `full_database_cold_load` vs Rust baseline (2929.900 ms) and Python baseline (3354.440 ms). Keep only if improvement justifies complexity.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
## Experiment: Parallel file IO (Rayon) in `Table::load_from_dir`
Approach: gather JSON paths, read+parse in parallel, then sequentially `put` + manifest updates.

### Bench results (10k `full_database_cold_load`, 5 runs)
- Rust (parallel attempt): mean **2934.240 ms**, median 2869.528
- Rust baseline (pre-change): 2929.900 ms (single run) → no meaningful improvement
- Python baseline: mean **3301.024 ms**, median 3126.695

### Decision
No measurable improvement vs Rust baseline; change adds dependency + complexity. **Reverted** the parallel load changes and removed Rayon.
